description: A hollow bottle-shaped vase, standing upright along the Z-axis, generated by revolving an explicit profile and shelling it inward with a 2mm wall thickness, leaving the top open.
notes: To model this, the Coder can either: 1. Create a 2D wire of the outer profile in the XZ plane, revolve it 360 degrees around the Z-axis to create a solid, and then shell the solid inward by 2mm while removing the top face (at Z=65). 2. Directly draw the 2D cross-section of the hollowed wall (including the 2mm base and 2mm walls) and revolve it 360 degrees. Method 1 (revolve solid then shell) is typically cleaner. Note that the top opening has an outer radius of 10mm and inner radius of 8mm (diameter 16mm).
